auth-email-password.whenToUse: Users sign in with email and password — login, signup, verification, and password reset on your apex surface or behind AuthGate in the admin app.

billing-foundation.whenToUse: Subscriptions run through Stripe or Mollie — billing-foundation hosts the provider plugins, normalizes webhook events, and writes tier state your app reads via tier-engine.

cap-counter.whenToUse: Usage must be metered per tenant and plan — wrap handlers with withCapEnforcement to soft-warn admins and hard-block overages against calendar-period counters.

channel-email.whenToUse: Email is a delivery channel for ctx.notify — mount channel-email on delivery with per-channel templates and a mail transport (SMTP or in-memory in tests).

compliance-profiles.whenToUse: Each tenant follows a different regulatory regime (EU, CH, HR) without forking app code — profiles drive retention, grace periods, and audit obligations.

config.whenToUse: Tenants need runtime-editable settings (API keys, branding, limits) with typed keys, role-gated writes, and optional encryption at rest.

custom-fields.whenToUse: Entities need tenant-defined extra columns without schema migrations — customFieldsField() stores typed extensions in jsonb with validation and CRUD wiring.

data-retention.whenToUse: Compliance profiles require automatic expiry of old rows — data-retention runs scheduled cleanup jobs per tenant profile without hand-rolled cron in your app.

delivery.whenToUse: You need to notify users across email, in-app, and push from domain handlers without wiring each channel yourself — mount delivery plus the channel features and declare templates.

file-foundation.whenToUse: File storage is pluggable (S3, in-memory, local) — file-foundation defines the provider extension point your files feature and upload handlers build on.

file-provider-inmemory.whenToUse: Tests and local dev need file upload without S3 — in-memory provider implements the file-foundation API for integration tests and demos.

files.whenToUse: Domain entities reference uploaded blobs via FileRef — the files feature handles metadata, signed URLs, and provider routing per tenant.

legal-pages.whenToUse: You ship a DACH-facing product and need Imprint and Privacy Policy as public HTML routes with a production boot check when required blocks are missing.

mail-foundation.whenToUse: Your app sends email through a pluggable transport — mail-foundation selects SMTP, in-memory, or a custom plugin per tenant via config without rewriting domain handlers.

mail-transport-inmemory.whenToUse: Tests and demos need outbound mail without SMTP — in-memory transport captures messages per tenant so integration tests assert subject/body without Mailpit.

managed-pages.whenToUse: Tenants need their own public marketing or help pages with branding, Markdown bodies, and optional tier-gated custom CSS.

secrets.whenToUse: API keys and tokens must live encrypted per tenant, not in env vars — secrets stores values in the DB with master-key encryption and config-gated reads.

sessions.whenToUse: Logins must be revocable server-side — logout, password change, or admin force-logout should invalidate outstanding JWTs before they expire.

tags.whenToUse: Users label records with a shared tag vocabulary — mount tags once and attach tag sets to any entity without a custom join table per feature.

tenant.whenToUse: Your app serves multiple organizations — tenants, memberships, and roles scope every query and write.

text-content.whenToUse: Marketing copy, help articles, and legal text should be editable per tenant without redeploying — keyed by slug and language.

tier-engine.whenToUse: Plans, caps, and paid feature toggles must follow a tenant's tier — including manual operator grants without a billing webhook.

user.whenToUse: One person can belong to multiple tenants with different roles — the user feature holds the cross-tenant identity.

user-data-rights.whenToUse: GDPR export, deletion, and restriction must work for your domain entities — register hooks once, the framework runs ZIP export and forget cleanup.

user-data-rights-defaults.whenToUse: Every app needs sensible GDPR defaults — this feature ships stock export/forget handlers and profile screens you extend with domain-specific EXT_USER_DATA hooks.

user-profile.whenToUse: Logged-in users change password, email, or schedule account deletion from a bundled self-service profile screen.
